current_implemented_build: V58.2 Runtime Action Queue to Proof Pack Trace Linkage
patch_type: Queue-to-proof trace linkage over V58.1 Runtime Action Queue
release_chain_status: V58.2 is the current implemented build. It loads after V58.1 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It connects Runtime Action Queue items to related decision records, evidence register items, Proof Pack sections, and board narrative sections while preserving no-new-top-level-tabs rule. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V58.2. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
v58_2_changes: adds queue-to-proof trace linkage surface into Proof Pack
adds queue-to-proof trace linkage surface into Runtime
adds trace-link modal showing linked decision records, evidence items, Proof Pack sections, and board narrative sections
enhances Runtime Action Queue cards with proof-trace access when matching links are available
preserves V58 Operational Trust Control Pane
preserves V57.1 Board and Executive Narrative Layer
preserves V56.4 Core Trust Scoring Models
preserves V56.3 Universal Score Object and Score Band Contract
preserves V56.1 Evidence and Assumption Register

queue_proof_trace_linkage.boundary: Static advisory prototype only. No live evidence, tickets, notifications, enforcement, or workflow integrations.

internet_trust_engine_future_track.sequence_note: V58.2 improves traceability needed before future Internet Trust Engine work, but does not implement live artifact analysis, live scoring, claim extraction, evidence retrieval, or internet verification.

recommended_next_build.label: V59 Internet Trust Engine MVP Scaffold
goals: introduce Internet Trust Engine as a scenario family and prototype scaffold
avoid live internet verification, live evidence retrieval, fact-checker branding, truth-engine branding, or political validation

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Queue-to-proof linkage uses static prototype and loaded in-browser registry data
Trace linkage does not retrieve live evidence
Trace linkage does not create tickets, send notifications, send reports, or trigger workflows
Trace linkage does not enforce, block, refuse, or quarantine actions in live systems
Trace linkage modal is advisory and not a production audit record
Internet Trust Engine is documented as a future requirement track but is not yet implemented
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
